KrutiDev to Unicode Converter (Free, Online, KrutiDev 010)

Paste your KrutiDev (or KrutiDev 010) Hindi text below and convert it to Unicode in one click — free, instant, and no login required. Works for Hindi and Marathi documents, government files, old DTP content, and anywhere you need text to display correctly on modern devices without installing a special font.

Reverse: Unicode to KrutiDev Need Chanakya font? KrutiDev to Chanakya Need Mangal? KrutiDev to Mangal

How to Convert Krutidev 010 to Unicode Devanagari

  1. Type or paste your legacy Krutidev 010 text in the left (or top on mobile) input text area.
  2. The converter will automatically process your input client-side in real time.
  3. Your converted standard Unicode Devanagari text will appear in the right (or bottom on mobile) output area.
  4. Click the Copy Text button to copy the output to your clipboard, or click Download to save it as a text file.

Detailed Guide: KrutiDev to Unicode Conversion

What Is KrutiDev to Unicode Conversion?

KrutiDev (also written Kruti Dev or KrutiDev 010) is a legacy non-Unicode Hindi font that has been used across Indian government offices, courts, typing institutes, and old DTP/print software since the 1990s. It maps Hindi glyphs onto English ASCII keys in a non-standard way, which means KrutiDev text only displays correctly if the exact same font is installed — on a phone, website, or any modern app without that font, it shows up as garbled symbols.

Unicode is the universal standard used by every modern device, browser, and operating system. Converting KrutiDev to Unicode takes that old ASCII-mapped text and rebuilds it as proper Devanagari Unicode — readable anywhere, searchable, copy-paste friendly, and compatible with Mangal, Nirmala UI, or any other Unicode Hindi font.

If you need the KrutiDev font installed on your system to read legacy documents before converting, you can download KrutiDev font for free from our resource page. For help with key mappings, see our KrutiDev keyboard layout chart.

This tool also correctly repositions characters like ikar (ि) and reph (र्), which sit in a different order in KrutiDev than they do in Unicode — a common source of broken conversions in cheaper tools.

Note: If your KrutiDev text currently displays as symbols like dsfjsd Á%hkjk█ in a plain text editor, that's expected — it just means the KrutiDev 010 font isn't applied. Paste it into the tool above as-is; you don't need to "fix" it first.

How to Convert KrutiDev to Unicode Online

  1. Paste your KrutiDev (KrutiDev 010) text into the input box above — copy it directly from Word, PDF, or wherever it currently lives.
  2. Click Convert to Unicode.
  3. Copy the Unicode output — it will display correctly in any standard font like Mangal or Nirmala UI, no special font needed.
  4. Paste it into Word, WhatsApp, a website, or any Unicode-compatible app.

For Excel files, paste each KrutiDev cell's content into the converter individually, or copy a column at a time — the tool handles multi-line text in a single pass.

KrutiDev to Unicode — Comparison Table

Term What it means
KrutiDev to Unicode Convert legacy KrutiDev font text → universal Unicode (Mangal-compatible)
KrutiDev 010 to Unicode Same thing — "010" is the specific, most common KrutiDev version
KrutiDev to Mangal Same result — Mangal is the most widely used Unicode Hindi font, so converting "to Unicode" and "to Mangal" produce the same text
Unicode to KrutiDev The reverse — see our Unicode to KrutiDev Converter

Who Needs to Convert KrutiDev to Unicode?

  • Government and court staff holding decades of old KrutiDev-formatted letters, judgments, and records who need to digitize them into a searchable, future-proof Unicode format.
  • Marathi and Hindi typists — KrutiDev was widely used for Marathi typing in Maharashtra government offices too, not just Hindi; this tool converts both correctly.
  • Students and typing exam candidates who learned on KrutiDev-based typing software but need Unicode output for online forms, applications, and modern documents.
  • Web publishers and bloggers with old KrutiDev-era Hindi content that displays as broken symbols on today's websites and needs to be restored to readable Unicode for SEO and accessibility.
  • Office and DTP professionals migrating old press/print files into modern Unicode-based publishing workflows.

Frequently Asked Questions

How do I convert KrutiDev to Unicode? ▼

Paste your KrutiDev text into the input box above and click Convert — the Unicode output appears instantly and is ready to paste anywhere.

Why does my KrutiDev text show as random symbols or boxes like █? ▼

That happens when KrutiDev-encoded text is viewed without the KrutiDev font installed — the device falls back to displaying raw character codes instead of Hindi glyphs. It's not corrupted; convert it here and it will display correctly in standard Unicode.

Is this converter free? ▼

Yes, completely free with no login, no word limit, and no hidden charges.

Does this work for Marathi text typed in KrutiDev? ▼

Yes. Marathi uses the Devanagari script, and KrutiDev was widely used for Marathi typing in Maharashtra government offices. This converter handles both Hindi and Marathi correctly, producing standard Devanagari Unicode.

Is KrutiDev still used today? ▼

Yes, in pockets — particularly older government offices, courts, and some typing exam syllabi — though most new systems have moved to Unicode. Converting old KrutiDev files to Unicode is how most people keep that content usable going forward.

What is Krutidev 010? ▼

Krutidev 010 is a popular legacy, non-Unicode Devanagari font. It uses the Remington typewriter layout mapping, which translates English keystrokes into Hindi characters visually.

What is Unicode? ▼

Unicode is the universal character encoding standard that represents Devanagari text natively on all modern digital platforms, search engines, and operating systems without needing custom font files.

Why convert Krutidev to Unicode? ▼

Krutidev text is unreadable on mobile phones, emails, or search engines unless a specific font is installed. Converting to Unicode standardizes the text, making it accessible, searchable, and web-ready.

What's the difference between KrutiDev and Mangal font? ▼

KrutiDev is a non-Unicode font that maps Hindi visuals onto English keys; Mangal is a Unicode font that follows the international Hindi text standard. Converting KrutiDev to Unicode effectively makes your text Mangal-compatible.

Are KrutiDev and Devanagari the same thing? ▼

No — Devanagari is the script (the writing system Hindi and Marathi use). KrutiDev and Mangal are both fonts that display Devanagari script, just using different underlying encodings.

Does this tool upload my text to a server? ▼

No. All conversion happens locally in your browser using JavaScript — your text never leaves your device, ensuring your documents remain completely private and secure.

Can I convert Unicode back to KrutiDev? ▼

Yes — use our Unicode to KrutiDev Converter for the reverse direction.

Will the converted Unicode text work in MS Word, Excel, and WhatsApp? ▼

Yes. Once converted, the text is standard Devanagari Unicode and works anywhere — Word, Excel, websites, WhatsApp, Google Docs, social media — no special font required.

Can I convert a Word, Excel, or PDF file directly? ▼

This tool converts pasted or typed text, not uploaded files. To convert a Word, Excel, or PDF document, open the file, copy the Krutidev text from it, paste it into the converter, and then paste the Unicode result back into your document.

How does the converter handle mixed Hindi and English text? ▼

Because legacy fonts like KrutiDev map Hindi characters directly onto English keys, any English words in your original KrutiDev document are represented under the hood by the same English keystrokes. The converter cannot distinguish between KrutiDev text and actual English text, meaning English words will also be converted and display as garbled Hindi. For mixed-language documents, you should copy and convert only the Hindi portions, or manually restore the English text after conversion.
